title: Fix label dimensions, barcode width, and scanning validation

requirements.text: Update label dimensions to 58mm width × 43mm height in the label settings store and ensure all CPCL generation uses these exact dimensions
acceptanceCriteria: Label settings store default dimensions are set to 58mm × 43mm
CPCL generator uses 58mm × 43mm for label boundaries

requirements.text: Adjust barcode width parameters to ensure both barcodes fit completely within the 58mm label width for scannable output, matching the layout shown in the uploaded reference photos where barcodes span nearly the full label width without clipping

quotes: just change barcode width to make sure whole barcode is fit on label so can be scanned
Make sure label look like on that photo

acceptanceCriteria: Both barcodes fit within 58mm label width with no clipping
Barcode width is adjusted in CPCL generation to maximize width while staying within boundaries
Generated labels match the visual layout of the reference photos (1771682557066499855940811118309.jpg, image-11.png, 17717694923414593234117505414352.jpg, image-12.png)
Printed barcodes are scannable without manual adjustment

requirements.text: When scanning the 1st or 2nd barcode in ScanPrintTab, if the scanned barcode has an incorrect prefix (not matching any configured prefix mappings), display an error message, play the error sound, and automatically clear the input box so the user can scan again immediately

quotes: When scanning 1st or 2nd barcode and if barcode got incorrect prefix except show error and making sound clear the box so can be scanned again

acceptanceCriteria: Error message displays when scanned barcode prefix does not match any configured prefix mapping
Error sound plays when incorrect prefix detected
Input box is cleared automatically after showing error
User can immediately scan again without manual intervention
Validation applies to both first and second barcode scans

requirements.text: Detect and show an error if the user scans the same serial number again (duplicate serial detection), preventing duplicate processing

quotes: Is user scan same serial again show error

acceptanceCriteria: System detects when the same serial number is scanned twice
Error message displays clearly indicating duplicate serial
Duplicate serial is not processed or printed
Error sound plays on duplicate detection

constraints: Maintain existing barcode validation and prefix mapping logic
Do not modify authentication or Internet Identity integration
Preserve existing label settings import/export functionality
nonGoals: Changing label type definitions (Dual Band, Tri Band, New Dual Band)
Modifying printer connection or USB transport logic
Altering backend schema or migration logic
Adding new barcode types beyond existing supported types
